title: The Opulence of the Absolute
introduction: Chapter 10 of the Bhagavad Gita, Vibhuti Yoga (The Yoga of Divine Opulences), is one of the most awe-inspiring and devotional chapters in the entire scripture. Having revealed His personal nature and the supremacy of devotion in Chapter 9, Krishna now responds to Arjuna’s request to describe His divine manifestations (vibhutis) — the most magnificent, glorious, and powerful expressions of Himself in the universe. This is Krishna’s loving answer to the devotee who longs to see and feel God everywhere. He lists dozens of His supreme opulences — the best, the first, the strongest, the most beautiful, the most essential in every category — so that Arjuna (and every reader) can train the mind to recognize the Divine presence in the grandeur of creation. For modern readers — living in a world of Instagram aesthetics, awe at nature documentaries, scientific wonder at the cosmos, and the search for meaning in everyday life — this chapter is a direct invitation: stop looking for God only in temples or meditation cushions; see Him in the majesty of mountains, the brilliance of the sun, the strength of leaders, the beauty of art, the intelligence of science, the love in relationships. The essence remains perfectly intact: Krishna is not distant — He is the source of all splendor, and by meditating on His opulences, the devotee’s heart becomes saturated with love, wonder, and constant remembrance of the Supreme Person.

shlokas.sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच |
भूय एव महाबाहो शृणु मे परमं वचः |
यत्तेऽहं प्रीयमाणाय वक्ष्यामि हितकाम्यया ||
shlokas.transliteration:
śrī-bhagavān uvāca
bhūya eva mahā-bāho śṛṇu me paramaṁ vacaḥ
yat te ’haṁ prīyamāṇāya vakṣyāmi hita-kāmyayā
shlokas.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: Again, O mighty-armed Arjuna, listen to My supreme word, which I shall impart to you for your benefit and which will give you great joy because you are My dear friend.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna lovingly invites Arjuna to hear more profound truths — because he's dear and sincere, the divine shares secrets for his highest good. This is intimate guru-disciple energy: trust, affection, and benefit. In life, the deepest wisdom often comes in close, loving relationships — mentors, friends, partners who want your growth. Open your heart to receive — sincerity attracts higher revelation. This chapter (Vibhūti Yoga) reveals divine glories — joy through awe and devotion.
shlokas.genz: Krishna's like 'yo one more time, strong-armed bro — listen to my deepest drop, I'm telling you this because you're my real one and I want you to level up big time.' Pure heart-to-heart energy — affection + benefit. Examples: when your closest homie shares the real tea because they care — that's this vibe. Sincere connection = deeper downloads. This chapter's about divine flexes — awe + devotion = joy unlock.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna says: 'Listen again, my strong brave friend — I'm going to tell you my most special words because I love you and want you to be very happy and safe.' It's like your best friend pulling you aside to share the biggest secret with lots of love — because they really care about you.

shlokas.sanskrit:
न मे विदुः सुरगणाः प्रभवं न महर्षयः |
अहमादिर्हि देवानां महर्षीणां च सर्वशः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
na me viduḥ sura-gaṇāḥ prabhavaṁ na maharṣayaḥ
aham ādir hi devānāṁ maharṣīṇāṁ ca sarvaśaḥ
shlokas.translation: Neither the hosts of demigods nor the great sages know My origin or opulences, for in every respect I am the source of the demigods and the sages.
shlokas.millennial: Even demigods and great sages don't fully know Krishna's origin and glories — He is the source of them all. Divine is beyond even highest beings' comprehension. Examples: no matter how advanced someone seems (scientists, gurus, leaders), the ultimate source is beyond intellect. Humility — even 'enlightened' ones are limited. Awe at divine mystery deepens devotion.
shlokas.genz: Even devas and maharṣis don't know My source or full power — I'm the origin of them all. Examples: no matter how high-level the beings — source is beyond their scope. Stay humble — even 'god-tier' knowledge is capped. Awe at the unknown fuels devotion.
shlokas.genalpha: Even the sky gods and very wise sages don't know where I come from or how powerful I really am — because I am the beginning of all of them. Like even the biggest stars and smartest teachers come from the same big light — but the light itself is much bigger and older than they can understand.

shlokas.sanskrit:
यो मामजमनादिं च वेत्ति लोकमहेश्वरम् |
असंमूढः स मर्त्येषु सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते ||
shlokas.transliteration:
yo mām ajam anādiṁ ca vetti loka-maheśvaram
asaṁmūḍhaḥ sa martyeṣu sarva-pāpaiḥ pramucyate
shlokas.translation: He who knows Me as the unborn, the beginningless, the Supreme Lord of all the worlds — he is undeluded among men and is freed from all sinful reactions.
shlokas.millennial: Knowing Krishna as unborn, beginningless, Lord of all worlds — frees from delusion and sin. Examples: deep understanding of divine eternity — removes confusion/fear. Liberation from guilt/karma through knowledge + devotion. Realization of timeless source brings clarity and freedom.
shlokas.genz: Know Me as unborn, no beginning, supreme boss of everything — no more illusion, all sins wiped. Examples: deep knowing of eternal source — confusion and guilt gone. Liberation through truth + love.
shlokas.genalpha: Whoever really knows Me as the one who never began, has no starting point, and is the big loving boss of all worlds — they become very clear inside and free from all bad feelings. Like understanding the sun never had a birthday — it always was — you feel safe and clear knowing the biggest light is forever.

shlokas.sanskrit:
बुद्धिर्ज्ञानमसंमोहः क्षमा सत्यं दमः शमः |
सुखं दुःखं भवोऽभावो भयं चैवाभयं च मे ||
shlokas.transliteration:
buddhir jñānam asaṁmohaḥ kṣamā satyaṁ damaḥ śamaḥ
sukhaṁ duḥkhaṁ bhavo ’bhāvo bhayaṁ caivābhayaṁ ca me
shlokas.translation: Intelligence, knowledge, freedom from doubt and delusion, forgiveness, truthfulness, self-control, calmness, pleasure and pain, birth, death, fear and fearlessness, nonviolence, equanimity, satisfaction, austerity, charity, fame and infamy — all these qualities of living beings are created by Me alone.
shlokas.millennial: All qualities — positive (intelligence, truth, calmness) and negative (pain, fear, infamy) — originate from divine. Examples: everything in human experience comes from higher source. No separation — accept all as part of divine play. Understand source of all — reduces judgment, increases compassion.
shlokas.genz: All traits — smarts, clarity, forgiveness, truth, control, peace, happy/sad, birth/death, fear/fearless — all from Me. Examples: every vibe/state originates from source. No split — all part of one energy. See divine in everything — no judgment.
shlokas.genalpha: All good and hard feelings — smart thinking, knowing, no confusion, forgiving, truth, control, calm, happy/sad, being born/dying, scared/brave — all come from Me. Like every color in a rainbow comes from the same light — all feelings and things come from the same big loving friend.

shlokas.sanskrit:
ऋषयः सप्त पूर्वे चत्वारो मनवस्तथा |
मद्भावा मानसा जाता येषां लोका इमाः प्रजाः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
ṛṣayaḥ sapta pūrve catvāro manavas tathā
mad-bhāvā mānasā jātā yeṣāṁ lokā imāḥ prajāḥ
shlokas.translation: The seven great sages and before them the four other great sages and the Manus [progenitors of mankind] come from Me, born of My mind, and all the living beings descend from them.
shlokas.millennial: Divine mind creates great sages (seven rishis, four Kumaras, Manus) — humanity descends from them. Examples: spiritual lineage from divine source. All wisdom traditions trace back to higher origin. Respect ancient knowledge — it's divinely inspired.
shlokas.genz: Source mind births seven great sages + four earlier ones + Manus — all beings come from them. Examples: OG spiritual OGs spawned from divine — human lineage from higher. Ancient wisdom = direct source download.
shlokas.genalpha: The seven wise grandpas and four even older wise ones, and the first family fathers — all came from My mind. All people in the world come from them. Like the first big wise thinkers and family starters were born from God's loving thoughts — and we all come from that big family tree.

shlokas.sanskrit:
एता विभूतयो योगं मम यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः |
सोऽविकल्पेन योगेन युज्यते नात्र संशयः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
etā vibhūtayo yogaṁ mama yo vetti tattvataḥ
so ’vikalena yogena yujyate nātra saṁśayaḥ
shlokas.translation: Whoever knows these divine opulences of Mine in reality is united with Me in unwavering devotion. There is no doubt about this.
shlokas.millennial: Truly knowing divine glories — unwavering union/devotion. No doubt. Examples: understanding divine manifestations deepens faith — steady bhakti. Knowledge + devotion = perfect yoga. Realization strengthens commitment.
shlokas.genz: Know My divine powers truly — unbreakable union/devotion. No cap. Examples: deep understanding of source flexes — rock-solid bhakti. Knowledge + love = perfect connection.
shlokas.genalpha: Whoever really understands these special big powers of Mine — they join with Me in strong loving yoga with no wobble. No doubt at all. Like really knowing how amazing your best friend is — you love them even more strongly and stay close forever.

shlokas.sanskrit:
मत्त एवैति तत्त्वानि सर्वाणि मम यो विदुः |
तत्त्वतो ज्ञायते येन तस्मिन् योगं युञ्जते ||
shlokas.transliteration:
matta eveti tattvāni sarvāṇi mama yo viduḥ
tattvato jñāyate yena tasmin yogaṁ yuñjate
shlokas.translation: One who knows that I am the source of all creation, the origin of everything, and that everything emanates from Me — he is fully engaged in My devotional service and is never deviated from the path of devotion.
shlokas.millennial: Know Me as source of all — everything comes from Me — leads to unwavering devotion. Examples: see divine as origin — natural bhakti flows. No deviation — steady path. Realization of source = perfect commitment.
shlokas.genz: Know Me as source of everything — all comes from Me — leads to unbreakable devotion. Examples: see source behind all — bhakti stays locked. No drift — steady grind.
shlokas.genalpha: Whoever knows I am the beginning of everything and everything comes from Me — they stay loving and close to Me with strong heart forever. Like knowing your mom is the one who made your whole family — you love her even more and stay close always.

shlokas.sanskrit:
अहं सर्वस्य प्रभवो मत्तः सर्वं प्रवर्तते |
इति मत्वा भजन्ते मां बुद्धिमन्तः समन्विताः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
ahaṁ sarvasya prabhavo mattaḥ sarvaṁ pravartate
iti matvā bhajante māṁ buddhimantaḥ samanvitāḥ
shlokas.translation: I am the source of all spiritual and material worlds. Everything emanates from Me. The wise who perfectly know this engage in My devotional service and worship Me with all their hearts.
shlokas.millennial: Divine as source of all worlds — everything comes from Me. Wise ones understand — full-hearted devotion. Examples: realize origin — natural loving worship. Heart + wisdom = perfect bhakti. See source everywhere — love flows.
shlokas.genz: I'm source of all realms — everything flows from Me. Real ones get it — full-heart devotion. Examples: see origin — automatic love/service. Wisdom + love = top tier bhakti.
shlokas.genalpha: I am where everything starts — all worlds and things come from Me. Smart people who really understand this love and serve Me with all their heart. Like knowing your mom is where your whole family began — you love her with everything.

shlokas.sanskrit:
मच्चित्ता मद्गतप्राणा बोधयन्तः परस्परम् |
कथयन्तश्च मां नित्यं तुष्यन्ति च रमन्ति च ||
shlokas.transliteration:
mac-cittā mad-gata-prāṇā bodhayantaḥ parasparam
kathayantaś ca māṁ nityaṁ tuṣyanti ca ramanti ca
shlokas.translation: The thoughts of My pure devotees dwell in Me, their lives are fully devoted to My service, and they derive great satisfaction and bliss from always enlightening one another and conversing about Me.
shlokas.millennial: Pure devotees: minds on Me, lives for Me, mutually enlighten, talk about divine — always satisfied/blissful. Examples: spiritual community — sharing stories, insights about higher — joy in connection. Satsang (truth company) — powerful practice. Build loving divine-centered relationships.
shlokas.genz: Real devotees: minds locked on Me, lives for service, enlighten each other, talk divine daily — constant satisfaction + bliss. Examples: divine community — sharing experiences, deep talks — pure joy. Satsang = high-vibe loop.
shlokas.genalpha: My loving friends keep thinking of Me, live to help Me, teach each other about Me, and talk about Me every day — they feel very happy and full of joy. Like best friends who always talk about their favorite person and help each other understand more — always smiling and happy together.

shlokas.sanskrit:
तेषां सततयुक्तानां भजतां प्रीतिपूर्वकम् |
ददामि बुद्धियोगं तं येन मामुपयान्ति ते ||
shlokas.transliteration:
teṣāṁ satata-yuktānāṁ bhajatāṁ prīti-pūrvakam
dadāmi buddhi-yogaṁ taṁ yena mām upayānti te
shlokas.translation: To those who are constantly devoted to serving Me with love, I give the understanding by which they can come to Me.
shlokas.millennial: To constant loving devotees — divine grants special intelligence to reach Him. Examples: sincere devotion — higher wisdom flows. Grace gives clarity/insight needed for union. Love attracts divine help — path opens.
shlokas.genz: To always-devoted lovers — I give the special smarts to reach Me. Examples: real love + consistency — divine downloads wisdom needed. Grace unlocks path. Bhakti = intelligence booster.
shlokas.genalpha: To people who love and serve Me every day with happy heart — I give them the special smart understanding so they can come close to Me. Like when you love your teacher very much — she gives you the best hints to understand everything easily.

shlokas.sanskrit:
तेषामेवानुकम्पार्थमहमज्ञानजं तमः |
नाशयाम्यात्मभावस्थो ज्ञानदीपेन भास्वता ||
shlokas.transliteration:
teṣām evānukampārtham aham ajñāna-jaṁ tamaḥ
nāśayāmy ātma-bhāva-stho jñāna-dīpena bhāsvatā
shlokas.translation: To show them special mercy, I, dwelling in their hearts, destroy with the shining lamp of knowledge the darkness born of ignorance.
shlokas.millennial: Divine compassion: living in devotees' hearts, destroys ignorance-darkness with knowledge-light. Examples: sincere seeker — inner illumination comes. Divine grace removes confusion — clarity dawns. Mercy for loving hearts — path lit.
shlokas.genz: Special grace for them — I live in their hearts, burn ignorance-dark with knowledge-light. Examples: real devotion — inner light switches on. Confusion cleared — clarity hits. Divine mercy for loving ones.
shlokas.genalpha: Because I love them so much, I live in their hearts and use a bright lamp of understanding to chase away the dark confusion from not knowing. Like when you love your child very much — you turn on the light in their room so they can see clearly and feel safe.

shlokas.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच |
परं ब्रह्म परं धाम पवित्रं परमं भवान् |
पुरुषं शाश्वतं दिव्यमादिदेवमजं विभुम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
arjuna uvāca
paraṁ brahma paraṁ dhāma pavitraṁ paramaṁ bhavān
puruṣaṁ śāśvataṁ divyam ādi-devam ajaṁ vibhum
shlokas.translation: Arjuna said: You are the Supreme Brahman, the ultimate, the supreme abode and purifier, the Absolute Truth, eternal divine person, the original Godhead, unborn, the greatest.
shlokas.millennial: Arjuna's declaration of faith: praises Krishna as supreme Brahman, abode, purifier, eternal divine person, original God, unborn, all-pervading. Examples: heartfelt recognition of divine greatness — devotion deepens. Affirmation strengthens connection. Speak your truth — relationship grows.
shlokas.genz: Arjuna's big love letter: 'You're supreme Brahman, ultimate home, purest, eternal divine person, original source, unborn, all-pervading.' Examples: pure praise — devotion level up. Speak your realization — bond strengthens.
shlokas.genalpha: Arjuna says: 'You are the biggest forever light, the safest home, the cleanest, the true forever person, the first God, never born, everywhere.' Like telling your best friend how amazing they really are — with all your heart.

shlokas.sanskrit:
आहुस् त्वामृषयः सर्वे देवर्षिर्नारदस्तथा |
असितो देवलो व्यासः स्वयं चैव ब्रवीषि मे ||
shlokas.transliteration:
āhus tvām ṛṣayaḥ sarve devarṣir nāradas tathā
asito devalo vyāsaḥ svayaṁ caiva bravīṣi me
shlokas.translation: All the great sages such as Nārada, Asita, Devala, and Vyāsa proclaim You as the Supreme Personality of Godhead. And now You Yourself are declaring it to me.
shlokas.millennial: Great sages confirm divine supreme nature — and Krishna confirms directly. Examples: ancient wisdom + personal revelation — double assurance. Trust lineage + direct experience. Build faith on both tradition and personal connection.
shlokas.genz: All OGs (Nārada, Asita, Devala, Vyāsa) say You're supreme — and now You're saying it Yourself. Examples: ancient receipts + direct DM — full confirmation. Trust lineage + personal vibe.
shlokas.genalpha: All the wise grandpas like Nārada, Asita, Devala, Vyāsa say You are the highest — and now You are telling me Yourself. Like all the best teachers agree You're the greatest, and then the greatest tells you directly — no doubt left.

shlokas.sanskrit:
सर्वमेतदृतं मन्ये यन्मां वदसि केशव |
न हि ते भगवन् व्यक्तिं विदुर् देवा न दानवाः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
sarvam etad ṛtaṁ manye yan māṁ vadasi keśava
na hi te bhagavan vyaktiṁ vidur devā na dānavāḥ
shlokas.translation: O Krishna, I totally accept everything You have told me as the truth. Neither the demigods nor the demons, O Lord, can understand Your personality.
shlokas.millennial: Arjuna accepts all as truth — even higher beings can't fully know divine personality. Examples: humility + faith — complete surrender. Divine mystery beyond intellect — devotion transcends understanding. Accept with trust — peace follows.
shlokas.genz: Arjuna's like 'I accept 100% everything You said as real.' Even gods/demons can't know Your full nature. Examples: humility + trust — full surrender. Mystery beyond brain — devotion wins. Accept — peace drops.
shlokas.genalpha: Arjuna says: 'I believe every word You said is true.' Even sky gods and strong demons can't understand how wonderful You really are. Like trusting your best friend completely — even if no one else gets how special they are, you know in your heart.

shlokas.sanskrit:
स्वयमेवात्मनात्मानं वेत्थ त्वं पुरुषोत्तम |
भूतभावन भूतेश देवदेव जगत्पते ||
shlokas.transliteration:
svayam evātmanātmānaṁ vettha tvaṁ puruṣottama
bhūta-bhāvana bhūteśa deva-deva jagat-pate
shlokas.translation: Indeed, You alone know Yourself by Your own Self, O Supreme Person, origin of all beings, Lord of all beings, God of gods, Lord of the universe!
shlokas.millennial: Only divine knows Himself fully — source of beings, Lord of beings, God of gods, universe Lord. Examples: self-knowledge of supreme is perfect — we know partially. Humility — divine mystery. Bow to the unknowable — devotion deepens.
shlokas.genz: Only You know Yourself completely — source, Lord of all, God of gods, universe boss. Examples: self-knowledge of source = perfect. We know little — stay humble. Mystery = devotion fuel.
shlokas.genalpha: Only You really know Yourself completely — You who make all things live, Lord of everything, God of all gods, boss of the whole world! Like only the sun really knows how bright and warm it is — we just feel it and love it.

shlokas.sanskrit:
वक्तुमर्हस्यशेषेण दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः |
याभिर्विभूतिभिर्लोकानिमांस्त्वं व्याप्य तिष्ठसि ||
shlokas.transliteration:
vaktum arhasy aśeṣeṇa divyā hy ātma-vibhūtayaḥ
yābhir vibhūtibhir lokān imāṁs tvaṁ vyāpya tiṣṭhasi
shlokas.translation: Please tell me in detail of Your divine opulences by which You pervade all these worlds and abide in them.
shlokas.millennial: Arjuna requests: tell fully Your divine glories by which You pervade and reside in all worlds. Examples: desire to know divine manifestations — deepens devotion/awe. Krishna lists vibhūtis next — see divine in everything. Cultivate vision of sacred in ordinary.
shlokas.genz: Arjuna's ask: 'Tell me full details of Your divine powers that fill and stay in all worlds.' Examples: wanting to see source in everything — devotion boost. Vibhūti list coming — see divine everywhere.
shlokas.genalpha: Arjuna asks: 'Please tell me everything about Your special big powers that fill every world and live inside them.' Like asking your superhero friend to show you all the amazing things they can do — so you can see how wonderful they are in everything.

shlokas.sanskrit:
कथं विद्यामहं योगिंस्त्वां सदा परिचिन्तयन् |
केषु केषु च भावेषु चिन्त्योऽसि भगवन्मया ||
shlokas.transliteration:
kathaṁ vidyām ahaṁ yogiṁs tvāṁ sadā paricintayan
keṣu keṣu ca bhāveṣu cintyo ’si bhagavan mayā
shlokas.translation: O Krishna, O Supreme Mystic, how shall I constantly meditate upon You? In what aspect should I think of You, O Supreme Personality of Godhead?
shlokas.millennial: Arjuna asks practical: how to constantly meditate on You? In which forms/aspects? Examples: guidance for daily practice — specific focal points. Krishna lists vibhūtis next — meditate on divine in best manifestations. Make devotion practical — choose inspiring aspects.
shlokas.genz: Arjuna's like 'how do I keep thinking of You always? Which parts/aspects should I focus on?' Examples: practical devotion guide — best focal points. Vibhūti list incoming — meditate on top manifestations. Make love actionable.
shlokas.genalpha: Arjuna asks: 'How can I always think of You? In which special ways or things should I think of You?' Like asking your favorite superhero: 'How can I keep you in my mind all day? Which of your cool powers should I picture?'

shlokas.sanskrit:
विस्तरेणात्मनो योगं विभूतिं च जनार्दन |
भूयः कथय तृप्यन्ति हि श्रोतुं तव मादनम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
vistareṇātmano yogaṁ vibhūtiṁ ca janārdana
bhūyaḥ kathaya tṛpyanti hi śrotuṁ tava mādanam
shlokas.translation: O Janārdana, again please describe in detail Your opulent power and Your mystic opulence, for I am never satiated in hearing Your nectar-like words.
shlokas.millennial: Arjuna requests more details of divine yoga and glories — never tired of divine nectar words. Examples: longing for more divine stories — devotional appetite. Hearing divine glories nourishes soul — never enough. Cultivate eagerness for spiritual knowledge.
shlokas.genz: Arjuna's like 'tell me more details about Your power and divine flexes — I never get enough of Your sweet words.' Examples: never satiated — devotional hunger. Hearing source stories = soul food. Keep wanting more truth.
shlokas.genalpha: Arjuna says: 'Please tell me more and more about Your special powers and magic — I never get tired of hearing Your sweet loving words.' Like asking your favorite storyteller to keep telling more wonderful stories because they make you so happy you can't stop listening.

shlokas.sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच |
हन्त ते कथयिष्यामि दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः |
याः काश्चित् पार्थ मुख्याः शृणु विश्वरूपिन् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
śrī-bhagavān uvāca
hanta te kathayiṣyāmi divyā hy ātma-vibhūtayaḥ
yāḥ kāścit pārtha mukhyāḥ śṛṇu viśvarūpin
shlokas.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: Yes, I will tell you of My splendorous manifestations, O best of the Bhāratas, but only of those which are prominent, for My opulence is limitless.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna agrees — will describe prominent divine glories (limitless overall). Examples: divine has infinite manifestations — focus on main ones for meditation. Listen with devotion — glories inspire awe/love. Practice: contemplate listed vibhūtis daily.
shlokas.genz: Krishna's like 'sure, I'll drop some of My main divine flexes — but they're endless.' Examples: infinite source powers — focus on key ones for meditation. Listen deeply — awe + love boost. Daily contemplation practice.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna says: 'Okay, I'll tell you about some of My most special and bright powers — but I have so many more!' Like a superhero starting to show you their coolest tricks — but they have endless more amazing ones.

shlokas.sanskrit:
अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थितः |
अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च ||
shlokas.transliteration:
aham ātmā guḍākeśa sarva-bhūtāśaya-sthitaḥ
aham ādiś ca madhyaṁ ca bhūtānām anta eva ca
shlokas.translation: I am the Self, O Guḍākeśa, seated in the hearts of all beings. I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of all beings.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna as Self in all hearts — beginning, middle, end of everything. Examples: divine is inner essence + source/sustainer/dissolver of all. See Self in own heart and all beings — unity + intimacy. Profound meditation focus — divine within.
shlokas.genz: I'm the Self in every heart — start/middle/end of all. Examples: source inside everyone — beginning, support, conclusion. See inner divine in self/all — unity + closeness. Deep meditation point.
shlokas.genalpha: I am the real inside Self living in every heart. I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of everything. Like the quiet loving light inside every person and the start, stay, and finish of every story — always there, always loving.

shlokas.sanskrit:
आदित्यानामहं विष्णुर्ज्योतिषामस्मि रविरंशुमान् |
मरिचिर्मरुतामस्मि नक्षत्राणामहं शशी ||
shlokas.transliteration:
ādityānām ahaṁ viṣṇur jyotiṣām asmi ravir aṁśumān
maricir marutām asmi nakṣatrāṇām ahaṁ śaśī
shlokas.translation: Of the Ādityas I am Viṣṇu, of lights I am the radiant sun, of the Maruts I am Marīci, and among the stars I am the moon.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna begins listing His prominent vibhūtis (divine manifestations): among Ādityas (solar deities) He's Viṣṇu, among lights the sun itself, among Maruts (wind gods) Marīci (chief), among stars the moon. These are supreme examples in each category — showing divine as the essence/best in everything. Examples: see divine in the most brilliant/powerful aspects of nature — sun's radiance, moon's calm glow. Daily life: feel awe at sunrise/sunset/moonlight — direct connection to higher. This builds devotion through wonder at divine presence in the world.
shlokas.genz: Krishna's first flexes: among Ādityas I'm Viṣṇu (the preserver), among lights the blazing sun, among Maruts Marīci (top wind god), among stars the moon. Peak in every category. Examples: divine = best version of everything — sun's full brightness, moon's chill glow. Look at sky — feel the source in the brightest/most beautiful. Vibes: nature worship through awe — direct divine hit.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna says: among the sun family I'm Viṣṇu (the big protector), among all lights I'm the bright sun, among wind gods I'm Marīci (the leader), among stars I'm the moon. I'm the very best and brightest in each group. Like being the shiniest star in the sky or the biggest sun — God is the most special part of everything beautiful we see.

shlokas.sanskrit:
वेदानाम् सामवेदोऽस्मि देवानामस्मि वासवः |
इन्द्रियाणां मनश्चास्मि भूतानामस्मि चेतना ||
shlokas.transliteration:
vedānām sāma-vedo ’smi devānām asmi vāsavaḥ
indriyāṇāṁ manaś cāsmi bhūtānām asmi cetanā
shlokas.translation: Of the Vedas I am the Sāma Veda; of the demigods I am Indra, the king of heaven; of the senses I am the mind; and in living beings I am the living force (consciousness).
shlokas.millennial: Divine as Sāma Veda (most musical/harmonious), Indra (king of gods), mind (chief sense), consciousness in beings. Examples: divine in harmony (music/prayer), leadership (authority), mind (thought), life force (awareness). Feel divine in music, decision-making, thinking, aliveness — everyday sacredness. Consciousness = divine spark in all.
shlokas.genz: Among Vedas I'm Sāma (melodic one), among gods Indra (top boss), among senses the mind, among beings consciousness itself. Examples: divine in good vibes/music, leadership energy, thinking power, pure awareness. Feel source in playlists, focus, inner aliveness — everyday divine hits.
shlokas.genalpha: Among holy books I'm the beautiful singing one (Sāma Veda), among sky gods I'm Indra the king, among senses I'm the thinking mind, among all living things I'm the alive feeling inside. Like being the nicest song, the strongest leader, the smart thoughts, and the happy awake spark in every friend and animal.

shlokas.sanskrit:
रुद्राणां शङ्करश्चास्मि विक्षोः पर्वतामस्मि मेरुः |
सुराणामस्मि वसवोऽस्मि ज्योतिषामस्मि भास्करः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
rudrāṇāṁ śaṅkaraś cāsmi vikṣoḥ parvatām asmi meruḥ
surāṇām asmi vasavo ’smi jyotiṣām asmi bhāskaraḥ
shlokas.translation: Of all the Rudras I am Lord Śiva, of the Yakṣas and Rākṣasas I am the lord of wealth Kuvera, of the Vasus I am fire (Agni), and of mountains I am Meru.
shlokas.millennial: Among Rudras (forms of Śiva) — Śaṅkara (auspicious Śiva), among Yakṣas/Rākṣasas — Kuvera (wealth), among Vasus — Agni (fire), among mountains — Meru (central cosmic mountain). Examples: divine as destroyer/transformer (Śiva), abundance (Kuvera), purifying fire (Agni), stability/center (Meru). See divine in transformation, wealth, purification, steadfastness.
shlokas.genz: Among Rudras I'm Śaṅkara (auspicious Śiva), among Yakṣas/Rākṣasas Kuvera (wealth boss), among Vasus Agni (fire), among mountains Meru (cosmic center). Examples: divine as change-maker (Śiva), abundance flow (Kuvera), purifying energy (Agni), rock-solid stability (Meru). Feel source in transformation, money, fire, mountains.
shlokas.genalpha: Among the strong Rudras I'm the kind Śiva, among treasure keepers I'm Kuvera the rich one, among good Vasus I'm the warm fire, among mountains I'm the biggest central Meru. Like being the powerful changer who brings good, the giver of treasures, the warm helpful fire, and the strongest steady mountain holding everything.

shlokas.sanskrit:
पुरोधसामपि मुख्यं मां विद्धि पार्थ बृहस्पतिम् |
सेनानीनामहं स्कन्दः सरसामस्मि सागरः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
purodhasām api mukhyaṁ māṁ viddhi pārtha bṛhaspatim
senānīnām ahaṁ skandaḥ sarasām asmi sāgaraḥ
shlokas.translation: Of priests, O Arjuna, know Me to be the chief, Bṛhaspati. Among military commanders I am Skanda, and of bodies of water I am the ocean.
shlokas.millennial: Among priests — Bṛhaspati (guru of gods), among army leaders — Skanda (Kārtikeya), among waters — ocean. Examples: divine as supreme teacher/wisdom (guru), victorious warrior (Skanda), vast/deep (ocean). See divine in wisdom/teaching, leadership/victory, depth/vastness.
shlokas.genz: Among priests I'm Bṛhaspati (top guru), among commanders Skanda (warrior god), among waters the ocean. Examples: divine as ultimate wisdom/teacher, victory/leadership, depth/vastness. Feel source in good advice, winning energy, deep oceans.
shlokas.genalpha: Among teachers and priests I'm Bṛhaspati the wisest, among army leaders I'm Skanda the brave, among all waters I'm the huge ocean. Like being the smartest teacher, the strongest general, and the biggest deepest sea full of life and mystery.

shlokas.sanskrit:
महर्षीणां भृगुरहं गिरामस्म्येकमक्षरम् |
यज्ञानां जपयज्ञोऽस्मि स्थावराणां हिमालयः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
maharṣīṇāṁ bhṛgur ahaṁ girām asmy ekam akṣaram
yajñānāṁ japa-yajño ’smi sthāvarāṇāṁ himālayaḥ
shlokas.translation: Of the great sages I am Bhṛgu; of vibrations I am the transcendental oṁ. Of sacrifices I am the chanting of holy names (japa), and of immovable things I am the Himālayas.
shlokas.millennial: Among great sages — Bhṛgu (foremost), among sounds — Om (primal vibration), among sacrifices — japa (mantra repetition), among immovables — Himālayas (steadfast). Examples: divine in ancient wisdom (Bhṛgu), sacred sound (Om), devotional repetition (japa), unshakeable stability (Himālayas). Chant Om, practice japa — feel divine in vibration/repetition/steadfastness.
shlokas.genz: Among maharṣis Bhṛgu (top), among sounds Om (transcendental), among yajñas japa (mantra rep), among immovables Himālayas. Examples: divine in ancient wisdom, primal frequency, devotional loops, rock-solid stability. Chant Om/japa — feel source in vibration/repetition/steadfastness.
shlokas.genalpha: Among the greatest wise people I'm Bhṛgu, among sounds I'm the holy 'Om', among prayers I'm the repeating holy names, among things that don't move I'm the great Himalayas. Like being the oldest wisest grandpa, the special magic sound, the happy repeating song, and the biggest strongest mountain.

shlokas.sanskrit:
अश्वत्थः सर्ववृक्षाणां देवर्षीणां च नारदः |
गन्धर्वाणां चित्ररथः सिद्धानां कपिलो मुनिः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
aśvatthaḥ sarva-vṛkṣāṇāṁ devarṣīṇāṁ ca nāradaḥ
gandharvāṇāṁ citrarathaḥ siddhānāṁ kapilo muniḥ
shlokas.translation: Of all trees I am the banyan tree, and of the sages among the demigods I am Nārada. Of the Gandharvas [heavenly musicians] I am Citraratha, and among perfected beings I am the sage Kapila.
shlokas.millennial: Among trees — banyan (vast, eternal), among divine sages — Nārada (devotee/storyteller), among Gandharvas — Citraratha (chief musician), among siddhas — Kapila (founder of Sāṅkhya). Examples: divine in expansive life (banyan), devotion/knowledge transmission (Nārada), beauty/music (Gandharvas), philosophical wisdom (Kapila). See divine in nature, devotion, art, philosophy.
shlokas.genz: Among trees banyan (vast root system), among deva-rishis Nārada (bhakti OG), among Gandharvas Citraratha (music boss), among siddhas Kapila (Sāṅkhya founder). Examples: divine in expansive/grounded life, devotion stories, music/beauty, deep philosophy. Feel source in nature, bhakti, art, wisdom.
shlokas.genalpha: Among all trees I'm the big banyan with huge roots, among sky sages I'm Nārada the storyteller, among music angels I'm Citraratha the leader, among perfect wise ones I'm Kapila the thinker. Like being the strongest tree that lives forever, the best story singer, the most beautiful music leader, and the deepest thinker.

shlokas.sanskrit:
उच्चैःश्रवसमश्वानां विद्धि माममृतोद्भवम् |
ऐरावतं गजेन्द्राणां वरुणं याससामहम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
uccaiḥśravasam aśvānāṁ viddhi mām amṛtodbhavam
airāvataṁ gajendrāṇāṁ varuṇaṁ yādasām aham
shlokas.translation: Of horses know Me to be Uccaiḥśravā, produced during the churning of the ocean for nectar. Of lordly elephants I am Airāvata, and among the aquatics I am Varuṇa.
shlokas.millennial: Among horses — Uccaiḥśravā (nectar-born), among elephants — Airāvata (Indra's), among aquatics — Varuṇa (god of waters). Examples: divine in majestic/beautiful/powerful beings — Uccaiḥśravā (speed/beauty), Airāvata (strength/royalty), Varuṇa (depth/ocean). See divine in nature's finest — awe inspires devotion.
shlokas.genz: Among horses Uccaiḥśravā (nectar-born), among elephants Airāvata (Indra's), among water beings Varuṇa (water lord). Examples: divine in top-tier majestic creatures — speed/beauty, power/royalty, depth/vastness. Feel source in nature's MVPs.
shlokas.genalpha: Among horses I'm Uccaiḥśravā the white super-fast one born from ocean nectar, among elephants I'm Airāvata the king elephant, among water creatures I'm Varuṇa the ocean lord. Like being the fastest prettiest horse, the strongest royal elephant, and the biggest deepest ocean ruler.

shlokas.sanskrit:
आयुधानामहं वज्रं धेनूनामस्मि कामधुक् |
प्रजनश्चास्मि कन्दर्पः सर्पाणामस्मि वासुकिः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
āyudhānām ahaṁ vajraṁ dhenūnām asmi kāma-dhuk
prajanaś cāsmi kandarpaḥ sarpāṇām asmi vāsukiḥ
shlokas.translation: Of weapons I am the thunderbolt, of cows I am the surabhi, of procreators I am Kandarpa (Cupid), and of serpents I am Vāsuki.
shlokas.millennial: Among weapons — vajra (Indra's thunderbolt), among cows — kāma-dhuk (wish-fulfilling), among procreators — Kandarpa (love/desire god), among serpents — Vāsuki (king serpent). Examples: divine as ultimate power (vajra), abundance/provider (wish-cow), creative love (Kandarpa), strength/poison control (Vāsuki). See divine in power, prosperity, love, mastery over danger.
shlokas.genz: Among weapons vajra (thunderbolt), among cows kāma-dhuk (wish-fulfiller), among creators Kandarpa (Cupid), among serpents Vāsuki (king). Examples: divine as max power, abundance/manifestation, creative desire, strength/control. Feel source in strength, provision, love, mastery.
shlokas.genalpha: Among weapons I'm the powerful thunderbolt, among cows I'm the wish-giving magical cow, among love-makers I'm Kandarpa (god of love), among serpents I'm Vāsuki the king serpent. Like being the strongest weapon, the cow that gives anything you wish, the god of sweet love, and the biggest strongest snake.

shlokas.sanskrit:
अनन्तश्चास्मि नागानां वरुणो यादसामहम् |
पितॄणामर्यमा चास्मि यमः संयमतांऽहम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
anantaś cāsmi nāgānāṁ varuṇo yādasām aham
pitr̥̄ṇām aryamā cāsmi yamaḥ saṁyamatāṁ aham
shlokas.translation: Of the many-hooded Nāgas I am Ananta, of the water beings I am Varuṇa, of the ancestors I am Aryamā, and among the maintainers of law I am Yama.
shlokas.millennial: Among Nāgas — Ananta (infinite), among aquatics — Varuṇa (water lord), among ancestors — Aryamā (chief), among punishers — Yama (death/justice). Examples: divine as infinite (Ananta), oceanic depth (Varuṇa), ancestral order (Aryamā), justice/law (Yama). See divine in vastness, depth, tradition, justice.
shlokas.genz: Among Nāgas Ananta (endless), among water beings Varuṇa, among ancestors Aryamā, among enforcers Yama. Examples: divine as infinity, ocean mastery, ancestral respect, justice/power. Feel source in vastness, depth, lineage, fairness.
shlokas.genalpha: Among big snakes I'm Ananta the infinite one, among water creatures I'm Varuṇa the ocean king, among ancestors I'm Aryamā the leader, among judges I'm Yama the fair one. Like being the never-ending snake, the big ocean boss, the grandpa leader, and the fair judge who keeps everything right.

shlokas.sanskrit:
प्रह्लादश्चास्मि दैत्यानां कालः कलयतामहम् |
मृगाणां च मृगेन्द्रोंऽहं वैनतेयश्च पक्षिणाम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
prahlādaś cāsmi daityānāṁ kālaḥ kalayatām aham
mṛgāṇāṁ ca mṛgendro ’haṁ vainateyaś ca pakṣiṇām
shlokas.translation: Among the demons I am the devoted Prahlāda, among subduers I am time, among beasts I am the lion, and among birds I am Garuḍa.
shlokas.millennial: Among Daityas — Prahlāda (pure devotee), among subduers — time (all-powerful), among animals — lion (king), among birds — Garuḍa (Viṣṇu's carrier). Examples: divine in pure devotion (Prahlāda), irresistible force (time), strength/royalty (lion), speed/loyalty (Garuḍa). See divine even in 'negative' categories through best examples.
shlokas.genz: Among demons Prahlāda (bhakti king), among conquerors time, among beasts lion, among birds Garuḍa. Examples: divine in pure devotion even among opposites, unstoppable force, kingly strength, speed/loyalty. Source in all categories through best forms.
shlokas.genalpha: Among demons I'm Prahlāda the super-loving devotee, among things that control I'm time, among animals I'm the lion king, among birds I'm Garuḍa the fast eagle. Like being the best loving kid in a naughty group, the unstoppable time, the strongest king animal, and the fastest loyal bird.

shlokas.sanskrit:
पवनः पवतामस्मि रामः शस्त्रभृतामहम् |
झषाणां मकरश्चास्मि स्रोतसामस्मि जाह्नवी ||
shlokas.transliteration:
pavanaḥ pavatām asmi rāmaḥ śastra-bhṛtām aham
jhaṣāṇāṁ makaraś cāsmi srotasām asmi jāhnavī
shlokas.translation: Of purifiers I am the wind, of the wielders of weapons I am Rāma, of fishes I am the shark, and of flowing rivers I am the Ganges.
shlokas.millennial: Among purifiers — wind (cleansing), among weapon-bearers — Rāma (ideal warrior), among fish — shark (strongest), among rivers — Ganges (most sacred). Examples: divine in purification (wind), righteousness/strength (Rāma), power (shark), purity/sanctity (Ganges). See divine in cleansing, justice, strength, holiness.
shlokas.genz: Among purifiers wind, among warriors Rāma, among fish shark, among rivers Ganges. Examples: divine as cleansing force, righteous power, top predator, sacred flow. Feel source in fresh air, justice, strength, holy rivers.
shlokas.genalpha: Among cleaning things I'm the fresh wind, among weapon holders I'm Rāma the perfect hero, among fish I'm the strong shark, among rivers I'm the holy Ganges. Like being the fresh breeze that cleans, the brave hero with bow, the strongest fish, and the most sacred flowing river.

shlokas.sanskrit:
सर्गाणामादिरन्तश्च मध्यं चैवाहमर्जुन |
अध्यात्मविद्या विद्यानां वादः प्रवदतामहम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
sargāṇām ādir antaś ca madhyaṁ caivāham arjuna
adhyātma-vidyā vidyānāṁ vādaḥ pravadatām aham
shlokas.translation: Of all creations I am the beginning and the end and also the middle, O Arjuna. Of all sciences I am the science of self-realization, and among logicians I am the conclusive truth.
shlokas.millennial: Divine as beginning/middle/end of creation, science of self (adhyātma-vidyā) among knowledges, conclusive argument among debaters. Examples: source of all processes, highest knowledge (self-realization), perfect logic. Prioritize self-knowledge — ultimate science. Seek truth in debates — divine in clarity.
shlokas.genz: I'm start/middle/end of all creations, self-knowledge among sciences, conclusive truth among arguments. Examples: source of everything, highest wisdom (self-realization), perfect logic. Focus on inner knowledge — real science. Seek clear truth.
shlokas.genalpha: I am the beginning, middle, and end of all things made, the science of knowing your real self among all learnings, and the clear true answer among all arguments. Like being the whole story from start to finish, the most important lesson about who you really are, and the right answer that wins every debate.

shlokas.sanskrit:
अक्षराणामकारोऽस्मि द्वन्द्वः सामासिकस्य च |
अहमेवाक्षयः कालो धाताहं विश्वतोमुखः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
akṣarāṇām a-kāro ’smi dvandvaḥ sāmāsikasya ca
aham evākṣayaḥ kālo dhātāhaṁ viśvato-mukhaḥ
shlokas.translation: Of letters I am a, of compound words I am the dual, I am inexhaustible time, and the sustainer whose face is turned in all directions.
shlokas.millennial: Among letters — 'a' (first), among compounds — dvandva (pair), inexhaustible time, sustainer facing all directions. Examples: divine as origin (first letter), balance/union (pairs), eternal time, all-pervading support. See divine in beginnings, harmony, time flow, universal support.
shlokas.genz: Among letters 'a' (alpha), among compounds dvandva (pair), endless time, sustainer facing everywhere. Examples: source as origin, balance/union, eternal flow, all-around support. Feel divine in starts, harmony, time, everywhere presence.
shlokas.genalpha: Among letters I'm the first 'a', among word pairs I'm the balanced pair, I'm the never-ending time, and the supporter who looks everywhere. Like being the first sound in every word, the perfect pair that matches, the forever ticking clock, and the big hug that holds everything from all sides.

shlokas.sanskrit:
मृत्युः सर्वहरश्चाहमुद्भवश्च भविष्यताम् |
कीर्तिः श्रीर्वाक् च नारीणां स्मृतिर्मेधा धृतिः क्षमा ||
shlokas.transliteration:
mṛtyuḥ sarva-haraś cāham udbhavaś ca bhaviṣyatām
kīrtiḥ śrīr vāk ca nārīṇāṁ smṛtir medhā dhṛtiḥ kṣamā
shlokas.translation: I am all-devouring death, and I am the generating principle of all that is yet to be born. Among women I am fame, fortune, speech, memory, intelligence, steadfastness, and patience.
shlokas.millennial: Divine as all-consuming death (end), generating principle (beginning), and feminine qualities: fame, fortune, speech, memory, intelligence, steadiness, forgiveness. Examples: divine in endings/beginnings, and best feminine traits. Balance masculine/feminine — see divine in power/gentleness. Appreciation of qualities as divine manifestations.
shlokas.genz: I'm all-devouring death (end), generating principle (new beginnings), and among women: fame, fortune, speech, memory, intelligence, steadiness, patience. Examples: divine in endings/startups, and top feminine powers. Balance strength/softness — see source in power/grace.
shlokas.genalpha: I am the death that takes everything at the end, and the new birth of everything coming, and among girls/women I'm fame, good luck, beautiful words, good memory, smart thinking, strong staying power, and forgiving patience. Like being the end that makes room for new, the beginning of new things, and all the best qualities that make girls special and strong.

shlokas.sanskrit:
बृहत्साम तथा साम्नां गायत्री छन्दसामहम् |
मासानां मार्गशीर्षोऽहमृतूनां कुसुमाकरः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
bṛhat-sāma tathā sāmnāṁ gāyatrī chandasām aham
māsānāṁ mārga-śīrṣo ’ham ṛtūnāṁ kusumākaraḥ
shlokas.translation: Of the hymns in the Sāma Veda I am the Bṛhat-sāma, and of poetic meters I am the Gāyatrī. Of months I am Mārga-śīrṣa [November–December], and of seasons I am spring, which brings flowers.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna continues revealing His glories: among Sāma Veda hymns the grand Bṛhat-sāma (most exalted), among meters the sacred Gāyatrī (most purifying), among months Mārga-śīrṣa (auspicious for rituals), among seasons spring (full of beauty and new life). Examples: divine in the most sublime music (Bṛhat-sāma), purifying chant (Gāyatrī), favorable time (Mārga-śīrṣa), renewal/beauty (spring). Feel divine in beautiful music, sacred sounds, auspicious moments, seasonal joy — everyday reminders of higher presence.
shlokas.genz: Among Sāma chants I'm the epic Bṛhat-sāma, among meters Gāyatrī (power chant), among months Mārga-śīrṣa (blessed window), among seasons spring (bloom season). Examples: divine in the grandest melody, most potent mantra, lucky timing, fresh bloom vibes. Feel source in fire playlists, powerful chants, good timing, spring energy — daily divine hits.
shlokas.genalpha: Among beautiful songs I'm the biggest grand one (Bṛhat-sāma), among special prayer rhythms I'm the holy Gāyatrī, among months I'm the lucky November-December, among seasons I'm spring full of flowers. Like being the most amazing song, the strongest magic prayer words, the best lucky month, and the happiest flower-filled springtime.

shlokas.sanskrit:
द्यूतं छलयतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् |
जयोऽस्मि व्यवसायोऽस्मि सत्त्वं सत्त्ववतामहम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
dyūtaṁ chalayatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham
jayo ’smi vyavasāyo ’smi sattvaṁ sattvatām aham
shlokas.translation: I am the gambling of the gamblers, the splendor of the splendid, victory of the victorious, the strength of the strong, and the goodness of the good.
shlokas.millennial: Divine as gambling among cheaters (chance/risk), splendor in brilliant, victory in winners, strength in strong, sattva (purity/goodness) in good people. Examples: divine in calculated risk (gambling), brilliance, success, power, virtue. See higher in excellence, purity, achievement — but without attachment to outcomes. Divine essence in positive qualities.
shlokas.genz: Among gamblers I'm the dice roll (chance), among shining ones the shine, among winners victory, among strong the strength, among good the goodness. Examples: divine in high-stakes risk, brilliance, W energy, power, pure vibes. Feel source in clutch moments, glow-ups, wins, strength, virtue.
shlokas.genalpha: Among people who gamble I'm the exciting chance, among bright shining people I'm the brightness, among winners I'm the winning, among strong I'm the strength, among good people I'm the goodness. Like being the lucky dice roll, the sparkly shine, the happy victory, the big power, and the kind pure heart.

shlokas.sanskrit:
वृष्णीनां वासुदेवोऽस्मि पाण्डवानां धनञ्जयः |
मुनीनामप्यहं व्यासः कवीनामुशना कविः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
vṛṣṇīnāṁ vāsudevo ’smi pāṇḍavānāṁ dhanañjayaḥ
munīnām apy ahaṁ vyāsaḥ kavīnām uśanā kaviḥ
shlokas.translation: Of the descendants of Vṛṣṇi I am Vāsudeva, of the Pāṇḍavas I am Arjuna, of the sages I am Vyāsa, and among great thinkers I am Uśanā (Śukrācārya).
shlokas.millennial: Among Vṛṣṇis — Vāsudeva (Krishna Himself), among Pāṇḍavas — Arjuna (greatest warrior), among sages — Vyāsa (compiler of Vedas), among poets/thinkers — Uśanā (wise guru). Examples: divine in His own form, in ideal disciple/warrior (Arjuna), in wisdom transmission (Vyāsa), in profound insight (Uśanā). See divine in exemplary figures and wisdom lineages.
shlokas.genz: Among Vṛṣṇis I'm Vāsudeva (Krishna), among Pāṇḍavas Arjuna (clutch warrior), among sages Vyāsa (knowledge OG), among thinkers Uśanā (wise poet). Examples: divine in own avatar, ideal student/warrior, wisdom compiler, deep insight. Feel source in legends and profound minds.
shlokas.genalpha: Among the Vṛṣṇi family I'm Vāsudeva (Krishna Himself), among Pāṇḍavas I'm Arjuna the brave, among wise sages I'm Vyāsa the great storyteller, among poets and thinkers I'm Uśanā the wise one. Like being the hero in your own family, the best friend in the group, the great teacher who writes the books, and the deepest poet.

shlokas.sanskrit:
दण्डो दमयतामस्मि नीतिरस्मि जिगीषताम् |
मौनं चैवास्मि गुह्यानां ज्ञानं ज्ञानवतामहम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
daṇḍo damayatāṁ asmi nītir asmi jigīṣatām
maunaṁ caivāsmi guhyānāṁ jñānaṁ jñānavatām aham
shlokas.translation: Among punishments I am the rod of chastisement, among those who seek victory I am morality, among secrets I am silence, and among the wise I am wisdom.
shlokas.millennial: Among punishers — rod (discipline), among victors — morality/strategy, among secrets — silence, among wise — wisdom itself. Examples: divine in just discipline, righteous victory, protective silence, pure knowledge. See divine in order, ethics, discretion, understanding. Balance strength with wisdom.
shlokas.genz: Among punishers the rod (discipline), among winners morality/strategy, among secrets silence, among wise wisdom. Examples: divine in fair correction, ethical wins, keeping quiet, deep knowing. Feel source in discipline, right path, privacy, clarity.
shlokas.genalpha: Among things that control I'm the fair stick of discipline, among those who want to win I'm the right way, among secrets I'm the wise silence, among smart people I'm the wisdom. Like being the fair teacher who corrects kindly, the right rules to win, the quiet safety of secrets, and the clearest understanding.

shlokas.sanskrit:
यच्चापि सर्वभूतानां बीजं तदहमर्जुन |
न तदस्ति विना यत्स्यान्मया भूतं चराचरम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
yac cāpi sarva-bhūtānāṁ bījaṁ tad aham arjuna
na tad asti vinā yat syān mayā bhūtaṁ carācaram
shlokas.translation: Furthermore, O Arjuna, I am the generating seed of all existences. There is no being — moving or nonmoving — that can exist without Me.
shlokas.millennial: Divine as seed of all existence — nothing moves or exists without Him. Examples: source of all life/energy — dependency on higher. See divine in every spark of life/motion. Complete dependence — humility + security. Nothing independent of divine.
shlokas.genz: I'm the seed of all beings — nothing exists/moves without Me. Examples: source code of everything — no independent existence. All life/energy from source. See divine in every vibe/movement.
shlokas.genalpha: I am the seed that makes all things grow — nothing moving or still can exist without Me. Like every plant, animal, person, and rock needs the big loving light to be — everything depends on Me to exist and live.

shlokas.sanskrit:
नान्तोऽस्ति मम दिव्यानां विभूतीनां परन्तप |
एष तूद्देशतः प्रोक्तो विभूतेर्विस्तरः मया ||
shlokas.transliteration:
nānto ’sti mama divyānāṁ vibhūtīnāṁ parantapa
eṣa tv uddeśataḥ prokto vibhūter vistaraḥ mayā
shlokas.translation: O chastiser of the enemies, there is no end to My divine manifestations. What I have spoken to you is but a mere indication of My infinite opulences.
shlokas.millennial: Divine glories infinite — this is just a brief indication. Examples: limitless manifestations — can't list all. Humility in knowing — even great revelations are partial. Inspires ongoing wonder/devotion — divine always more to discover.
shlokas.genz: My divine flexes have no end — this is just a teaser/sample. Examples: infinite source powers — can't cover all. Stay humble — even big drops are partial. Endless awe + devotion fuel.
shlokas.genalpha: My special big powers have no end — what I told you is just a little bit to give you an idea. Like showing a few amazing tricks but having millions more — there's always more wonder and love to discover.

shlokas.sanskrit:
यद्यद्विभूतिमत्सत्त्वं श्रीमदूर्जितमेव वा |
तत्तदेवावगच्छ त्वं मम तेजोंऽशसम्भवम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
yad yad vibhūtimat sattvaṁ śrīmad ūrjitam eva vā
tat tad evāvagaccha tvaṁ mama tejo-’ṁśa-sambhavam
shlokas.translation: Know that all opulent, beautiful and glorious creations spring from but a spark of My splendor.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna summarizes: any being/object that possesses opulence, beauty, glory, power — recognize it as a mere fragment of My divine splendor. This is the key takeaway from vibhūti yoga — see divine in excellence everywhere. Examples: a brilliant leader, stunning artwork, powerful technology, majestic nature — all reflections of a tiny portion of divine energy. Cultivate this vision daily — transforms ordinary admiration into devotion and gratitude. No need to list infinitely — the principle applies universally: wherever you see greatness, see a spark of the Supreme.
shlokas.genz: Krishna's final flex summary: anything lit, beautiful, powerful, glorious — know it's just a tiny spark from My energy. Examples: viral creator's charisma, insane tech, breathtaking view, clutch performance — all mini drops of source power. See divine in every glow-up — no need for full list. Wherever excellence hits, spot the source fragment. Instant devotion hack — turn admiration into worship.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna says: whenever you see something very special, beautiful, strong, or full of light and glory — know that it's just a little spark from My big bright power. Like seeing a super shiny toy, a beautiful flower, a strong animal, or someone doing amazing things — all of them have a tiny piece of God's magic light inside. Look for that sparkle everywhere and feel happy knowing it's from the big loving friend.

shlokas.sanskrit:
अथवा बहुनैतेन किं ज्ञातेन तवार्जुन |
विष्टभ्याहमिदं कृत्स्नमेकांशेन स्थितो जगत् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
atha vā bahunaitena kiṁ jñātena tavārjuna
viṣṭabhyāham idaṁ kṛtsnam ekāṁśena sthito jagat
shlokas.translation: But what need is there, Arjuna, for all this detailed knowledge? With a single fragment of Myself I pervade and support this entire universe.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna concludes with humility and wonder: why need so many details? Just one fraction of My being pervades and sustains the whole universe. Examples: infinite divine can't be fully listed — even a tiny portion holds everything together. This inspires awe and surrender — no need to comprehend all, just recognize the vastness and trust. Shift from intellectual pursuit to loving devotion — the essence is enough. Divine is both immanent (in all) and transcendent (beyond all).
shlokas.genz: Krishna's mic-drop: 'why need all these examples? Just one tiny piece of Me holds up the entire universe.' Examples: infinite source — can't list everything. One fragment runs the whole show. Feel the awe — drop need for full explanation. Surrender + wonder = real connection. Divine everywhere and way beyond.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna says: 'But why talk so much? Just one small part of Me holds and keeps alive this whole big world.' Like knowing that even though the ocean is huge, just one little drop has the same salty taste and power — God is so big that even a tiny piece of Him keeps everything safe and moving. You don't need to know everything — just feel the wonder and love.
